headline: SpaceX, Other Mega IPOs Denied Fast Index Entry by S&P
summary: (Bloomberg) -- S&P Dow Jones Indices will keep its existing eligibility requirements for benchmarks including the S&P 500, closing the door to fast entry for big tech IPOs like SpaceX and delaying billions of dollars in flows from passive funds.Most Read from BloombergSpaceX, Other Mega IPOs Denied Fast Index Entry by S&PSaylor’s Bitcoin Machine Is Misfiring on Every CylinderGlazer Family Members Study Manchester United Stake SaleRepublican-Led House Votes to Stop Iran War, Rebuking TrumpBloombe

headline: Did SEC-Approved QBTC Bitcoin Index Options Just Recast Nasdaq's (NDAQ) Market-Infrastructure Narrative?
summary: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission recently approved Nasdaq’s plan to list cash-settled Bitcoin index options on the Philadelphia Stock Exchange under the QBTC ticker, offering European-style contracts that settle in cash against a Bitcoin index rather than physical BTC. This framework gives institutions and sophisticated traders a regulated way to express Bitcoin views and manage risk using familiar listed options infrastructure, potentially tightening the link between traditional...

headline: SpaceX IPO Pitch Targets $1.8 Trillion Valuation On 100x AI Growth
summary: Wall Street models SpaceX AI revenue rising sharply as the company markets a $75 billion IPO.
